acceptance_conditions.condition: contract, protocol, accepted R152 result, frozen routes, denominators, and source hashes remain exact
three groups produce 96 rows and 288 executions in four independent eight-row blocks per group
all six frozen repaired and denominator routes retain semantic fidelity at least 0.9999999999
portfolio repaired-automatic mean is at least -0.005 and bootstrap lower at least -0.01
portfolio repaired-denominator mean is at least -0.005 and bootstrap lower at least -0.015
all three backend groups have mean repaired-denominator delta at least -0.02
zero rows have repaired-denominator regression below -0.05
Casablanca clears -0.02, at least 10 of 12 blocks clear -0.03, and each backend block spread is at most 0.08
challenge commitment, hidden rows, reveal, row hashes, and transcript replay
hardware, causal, temporal, real-device transfer, general generation, advantage, BQP, solved-frontier, and credit claims remain false

challenge_protocol.automatic_rule: fresh optimization-level-3 compile with a hidden transpiler seed
challenge_protocol.denominator_rule: replay the frozen R150 minimum-calibration-exposure route from 80 independent optimization-level-3 transpiler seeds
challenge_protocol.repaired_rule: replay the accepted R152 Casablanca novel-signature route and the accepted Nairobi/Perth control routes without selection or fitting
challenge_protocol.shared_seed_rule: all three arms in one row share one simulator seed

claim_boundary.what_is_not_supported_even_if_accepted: causal repair, temporal transfer, real-device transfer, hardware performance, general route-generation advantage, quantum advantage, BQP separation, or solved B4/B8/B10

phase_protocol: commit a fresh secret after public preregistration
derive independent hidden transpiler, simulator, and bootstrap seeds
replay the frozen repaired, strong-denominator, and fresh automatic arms
write all 96 rows before secret reveal
reveal and verify group, block, and portfolio acceptance conditions
research_question: Does the accepted R152 finite repair survive four independent hidden seed blocks per backend without changing any route?
